_comment: Functional verifications: a rule's check/remediate/rollback proven to work on a live OS, independent of any framework benchmark. Scope: check | remediate | rollback | full. This is recorded fact, not inference — each entry corresponds to an actual live run.

verifications.notes: read-only check, PASS (chrony present)
read-only check, PASS (rsh-server absent)
read-only check, PASS (openssh-server present)
read-only check, FAIL correct (auditd absent on 26.04); confirms the audit->auditd name-fix queries the right package
read-only check, PASS (libpam-pwquality present); confirms the libpwquality->libpam-pwquality name-fix
read-only check, PASS (nfs-kernel-server absent); confirms the nfs-utils->nfs-kernel-server name-fix
read-only check, PASS (telnetd absent); confirms the telnet-server->telnetd name-fix
check FAIL -> remediate FIXED (apt install auditd 1:3.1.2) -> rollback restored (package removed). apt is cli-best-effort: rollback left /etc/audit config residue (dpkg rc state), not byte-perfect.
read-only check, FAIL correct (apparmor absent in minimal container); queries the right package
read-only check, FAIL correct (ufw absent)
read-only check, PASS (ntp absent)
check FAIL -> remediate FIXED (apt install vlock 2.2.2) -> rollback succeeded (package removed). apt_present full cycle.
check FAIL (present) -> remediate FIXED (apt remove systemd-timesyncd). apt_absent apply proven; rollback not exercised.
read-only check, PASS (kernel.dmesg_restrict=1); extend of the RHEL sysctl rule, mechanism OS-agnostic
read-only check, PASS (net.ipv4.tcp_syncookies=1)
read-only check, PASS (kernel.randomize_va_space=2)
read-only check, PASS (ctrl-alt-del.target disabled); service mechanism works on Ubuntu systemd
read-only check, FAIL correct (auditd not-found; auditd absent on host)
read-only check, FAIL correct (/var/log/audit absent; gated by depends_on auditd-enabled)
check FAIL -> remediate FIXED (wrote /etc/modprobe.d blacklist) -> rollback removed it BYTE-PERFECT (file create/remove, not cli-best-effort)

read-only check, FAIL correct (/usr/bin/journalctl is 0755, rule wants 0740; host not hardened to this control). Confirms file_permission evaluates the right path+mode on Ubuntu; file_permissions apply/rollback is byte-perfect kernel-atomic (M2 reference handler).
read-only check, PASS (/var/log/syslog mode 0640)
read-only check, PASS (/var/log/syslog owner syslog)
read-only check, PASS (/var/log/syslog group adm)
read-only check, PASS (/var/log owner root)
read-only check, PASS (/var/log group syslog — Ubuntu-specific; RHEL var-log-dir-permissions uses group root)
read-only check, FAIL correct (/var/log is 775, rule wants 0755; host not hardened). Confirms file_permission evaluates the right path+mode on Ubuntu.

read-only check (--sudo), FAIL correct (effective ciphers include chacha20-poly1305 + aes192-ctr, non-FIPS for Ubuntu). Live run CAUGHT a false-PASS bug: the command check used the bashism '&>/dev/null', which dash (Ubuntu /bin/sh) mis-parses as background+redirect -> check exited 0 regardless; rewritten POSIX-clean. PASS branch proven against the compliant Ubuntu cipher set under dash.
read-only check (--sudo), FAIL correct (effective MACs include umac-* and hmac-sha1, non-FIPS). Same bashism fix; PASS branch proven against the compliant MAC set under dash.

read-only check (--sudo), FAIL correct (effective kex include mlkem768/sntrup761/curve25519, non-FIPS). PASS branch proven for both the u24 superset and the u22 4-algo subset against the u24 allowlist (one rule covers both releases).
read-only check (--sudo), PASS (x11uselocalhost=yes). Extend of the RHEL rule to Ubuntu; sshd_effective_config method is native (no bashism).
read-only check (--sudo), PASS (permitemptypasswords=no). Extend of the RHEL rule to Ubuntu (V-270717).
read-only check (--sudo), PASS (permituserenvironment=no). Extend of the RHEL rule to Ubuntu (V-270717, second setting).

read-only check (--sudo), FAIL correct (dcredit not set in /etc/security/pwquality.conf; host does not enforce complexity). Extend of the RHEL rule (V-270728); config_value reads the right path/key on Ubuntu, config_set remediation is byte-perfect.
read-only check (--sudo), FAIL correct (ucredit not set). Extend (V-270726).
read-only check (--sudo), FAIL correct (lcredit not set). Extend (V-270727).
read-only check (--sudo), FAIL correct (ocredit not set). Extend (V-270733).
read-only check (--sudo), FAIL correct (minlen not set; built-in default 15 matches the Ubuntu requirement). Extend (V-270732).
read-only check (--sudo), PASS (PubkeyAuthentication=yes). Extend (V-270722, Ubuntu smart-card MFA control sets PubkeyAuthentication yes — same value).
read-only check (--sudo), PASS (UsePAM=yes). Extend (V-270741).
read-only check (--sudo), FAIL correct via comparator (PASS_MAX_DAYS=99999, expected <=60). Extend (V-270731); confirms config_value comparator works on Ubuntu login.defs.
read-only check (--sudo), FAIL correct via comparator (PASS_MIN_DAYS=0, expected >=1). Extend (V-270730).
read-only check (--sudo), FAIL correct (clientalivecountmax=3, expected 1; host default). Extend (V-270742).
read-only check (--sudo), FAIL correct (deny not set in faillock.conf; host not hardened). Extend (V-270690).
read-only check (--sudo), FAIL correct (dictcheck not set). Extend (V-270704).
read-only check (--sudo), FAIL correct (retry not set). Extend (V-270705).
read-only check (--sudo), FAIL correct (difok not set). Extend (V-270729).
read-only check (--sudo), FAIL correct (UMASK not set in login.defs; rule default 077 matches Ubuntu requirement). Extend (V-270716).
read-only check (--sudo), FAIL correct (auditd.conf absent on host; no auditd). Extend (V-270819, value root matches).

read-only check (--sudo), PASS (rsyslog enabled+active). Extend (V-270653 install+enable; the service-enabled aspect honestly covers 'preserve log records from failure events'). audispd-plugins V-270658 deferred with the audit cluster (needs audit subsystem, absent on 248).
read-only check (--sudo), PASS (no *.so in /lib /usr/lib more permissive than 0755; 2903 files scanned). Extend (V-270696); find + *.so filter match the Ubuntu check exactly.
read-only check (--sudo), PASS (all *.so owned root). Extend (V-270697).
read-only check (--sudo), PASS (all *.so group root). Extend (V-270699).
read-only check (--sudo), PASS (lib dirs owned root). Extend (V-270698).
read-only check (--sudo), PASS (lib dirs group root). Extend (V-270700).
read-only check (--sudo), PASS (no command in /usr/bin etc. more permissive than 0755). Extend (V-270701); /bin /sbin covered via usrmerge symlinks to /usr/bin /usr/sbin (verified on 248).
read-only check (--sudo), PASS (system commands owned root). Extend (V-270702).
read-only check (--sudo), PASS (system commands group root). Extend (V-270703).

read-only check (--sudo), PASS (/usr/bin etc. are 0755). Net-new (V-270824); command directories, -type d. /bin /sbin are usrmerge symlinks (contribute nothing under find -P), so the scan covers /usr/bin /usr/sbin /usr/local/*.
read-only check (--sudo), PASS (command dirs owned root). Net-new (V-270825).
read-only check (--sudo), PASS (command dirs group root). Net-new (V-270826).

checked standalone (depends_on auditd-enabled filtered as external) on a disposable Ubuntu 24.04 container with the auditd package installed (tool binaries present). PASS at 0755; NEGATIVE test confirms real evaluation: chmod 0777 /sbin/auditctl -> FAIL, restore 0755 -> PASS. Extend (V-270821).
checked standalone on the auditd container, PASS (tools owned root). Extend (V-270822).
checked standalone on the auditd container, PASS (tools group root). Extend (V-270823; no u22 equivalent control).

verifications.notes: FULL cycle on a real mutable-auditd host (enabled=1): check FAIL (not loaded) -> remediate FIXED via agent-mode AUDIT_NETLINK (loaded + persisted) -> check PASS + auditctl -l confirms -> rollback succeeded -> watch unloaded + persist file removed BYTE-PERFECT. First live audit_rule_set validation.
